metadata.summary: Fast, disk-efficient package manager using a content-addressable store and symlinks for strict dependency isolation. Always --frozen-lockfile in CI, pin manager version, block accidental npm/yarn use.

content.title: pnpm Package Management
content.summary: Fast, disk-efficient package manager using a content-addressable store and symlinks for strict dependency isolation. The concrete rule: always --frozen-lockfile in CI, pin manager version via "packageManager": "pnpm@9.x.y" in root package.json, and block accidental npm/yarn use via a preinstall script. shamefully-hoist=true is the universal wrong fix — reject any PR adding it without justification.

why.p: pnpm eliminates phantom dependencies (imports that work locally, fail in CI) by enforcing strict node_modules layout. The content-addressable store shares packages across projects, cutting disk use 50-90% on monorepos. --filter '...[origin/main]' enables building only changed packages in monorepos — the primary CI speed win over npm/yarn.

list.item: All new Node.js/TypeScript projects unless org policy requires npm/yarn
Monorepos with 3+ packages: pnpm workspaces are faster and first-class
CI pipelines where install time matters; pnpm + store cache shaves minutes
Projects with phantom-dependency bugs (imports work locally, fail elsewhere)
Container builds wanting layer-cached deps via pnpm fetch
React Native projects pinned to npm/yarn by Metro/Expo (Expo SDK <= 50 had pnpm rough edges)
One-file scripts where npm exec/bunx is lighter
Deploy targets with configs expecting package-lock.json that cannot be updated
Hosting platforms that don't support pnpm out of the box without explicit packageManager field (rare in 2026)

section.name: installation
section.p: Enable via corepack (Node.js 16.9+): corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate
Or: npm install -g pnpm

rule.statement: Pin manager version in root package.json: "packageManager": "pnpm@9.x.y". This ensures corepack picks the same version everywhere — CI, dev, Docker.
rule.rationale: Lockfile format changes between pnpm major versions. Without pinning, different developers and CI use different versions, producing lockfile churn that obscures real dependency changes.

section.name: npmrc-configuration
section.reference: Strict .npmrc config (engine-strict, frozen lockfile, no shameful hoist)

rule.statement: Keep shamefully-hoist=false (the default). Reject any PR setting shamefully-hoist=true without a 2-line justification. It defeats pnpm's phantom-dependency protection.
rule.rationale: shamefully-hoist=true is the universal agent wrong-fix when a native add-on or bundler can't find a package. The real fix is adding the package as an explicit dependency. Hoisting masks the dependency gap and spreads phantom imports.

section.p: Key .npmrc settings: strict-peer-dependencies=true, auto-install-peers=true, lockfile=true, prefer-frozen-lockfile=true, engine-strict=true.

rule.statement: Always use --frozen-lockfile in CI. Never --no-frozen-lockfile "to make it work". Lockfile drift must be fixed locally, not suppressed in CI.
rule.rationale: --no-frozen-lockfile in CI allows silent dependency drift that breaks reproducible builds. Any drift should be diagnosed and committed locally first.

rule.statement: Commit pnpm-lock.yaml separately from application code changes. This keeps the lockfile diff reviewable and enables targeted revert.
rule.rationale: Mixing lockfile and app changes in one commit makes it impossible to identify which dependency change caused a regression.

section.name: block-accidental-npm-yarn
section.p: Add to root package.json scripts: "preinstall": "npx -y only-allow pnpm"
This fails early when npm install or yarn is run accidentally, preventing mixed lockfile corruption.

section.name: workspace-setup
section.p: Root package.json: private: true, scripts running pnpm -r build / test / lint across all packages.

rule.statement: Use workspace:* for all internal dependency references. pnpm publish rewrites workspace:* to actual resolved versions at publish time — no manual conversion needed.
rule.rationale: workspace:* ensures internal packages always use the local version during development and get correct semver ranges in the published artifact.

section.name: filter-commands
section.p: pnpm --filter web build — run in specific package by name.
pnpm --filter "@mycompany/*" build — run in all packages matching scope.
pnpm --filter web... build — run in package and all its dependencies.
pnpm --filter "...[origin/main]" build — build only packages changed since origin/main. Major CI win for monorepos.
pnpm -r build — run in all packages in topological order (respects dep graph).
pnpm -r --parallel build — run in all packages in parallel (ignore dep order).

section.name: dependency-management
section.p: Add: pnpm add express zod / pnpm add -D typescript @types/node / pnpm add --save-peer react
Add workspace dep: pnpm --filter web add @mycompany/ui --workspace
Update: pnpm update --latest (ignore semver) / pnpm update -i (interactive)
Show outdated: pnpm outdated / pnpm outdated -r --format json (for agent consumption)

rule.statement: Run pnpm dedupe after major upgrades. Without it, the lockfile accumulates duplicate package versions that slow installs and increase bundle size.
rule.rationale: Agents forget dedupe. Over time, the lockfile bloats with multiple versions of the same package at slightly different patch versions.

section.name: store-management
section.p: pnpm store prune — remove unreferenced packages. pnpm store status — verify integrity. pnpm fetch — populate store without install (Docker layer pre-warming).
Cache store separately in CI: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store/v3 (not node_modules).
section.reference: Multi-stage Dockerfile using pnpm fetch for layer caching
GitHub Actions workflow with pnpm store cache

section.name: publishing
section.p: pnpm publish / pnpm -r publish for workspace. Use Changesets for version management: pnpm add -Dw @changesets/cli, pnpm changeset (create), pnpm changeset version (update), pnpm -r publish.

rule.statement: After pnpm patch, commit both the patch file and the pnpm.patchedDependencies field in package.json in the same commit.
rule.rationale: The patch file alone without package.json registration is ignored; the package.json update alone without the patch file fails install. Both are required atomically.

antipattern.description: Setting shamefully-hoist=true as a quick fix for "module not found" errors.
antipattern.reason: Defeats pnpm's core value proposition. The real fix is adding the missing package as an explicit dependency. Hoisting hides the gap and spreads phantom imports to other consumers.

antipattern.description: Mixing npm install or yarn add commands with pnpm in the same project.
antipattern.reason: Creates a second lockfile (package-lock.json or yarn.lock), corrupts node_modules layout, and produces non-reproducible installs. The preinstall hook with only-allow pnpm prevents this.

antipattern.description: Using npx instead of pnpm dlx for one-shot package execution.
antipattern.reason: Works but bypasses pnpm's content-addressable store, creating a separate npm-managed cache and potentially using a different version than workspace deps.

antipattern.description: Not committing pnpm-lock.yaml to source control.
antipattern.reason: Without the lockfile, installs are not reproducible. Every install may resolve to different patch versions, silently changing behavior across environments.

section.name: native-addon-exception
section.p: Some native add-ons (sharp, node-canvas) require flat node_modules to find system libraries. In this case, add node-linker=hoisted to .npmrc and document the reason explicitly. This is the justified exception to shamefully-hoist.

example.code: # .npmrc — node-linker=hoisted required for sharp native bindings # See: https://github.com/lovell/sharp/issues/2541 node-linker=hoisted
example.note: Always add a comment with the reason and linked issue. This makes the exception reviewable and removable when the upstream fix lands.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A package agent reads package.json + pnpm-workspace.yaml → runs pnpm install --frozen-lockfile → executes pnpm -r build && pnpm -r test → on failure, narrows with --filter to the breaking workspace. For dep upgrades, run pnpm outdated -r --format json, feed to LLM for risk classification (major/minor/patch), then pnpm update -r --interactive only on approved set. Always commit pnpm-lock.yaml separately from app code.
section.prompt: Audit dependencies: run `pnpm outdated -r --format json`. Classify each: patch=auto, minor=review, major=manual. Output: 3 separate batches as bash commands using `pnpm update --filter=<pkg> <dep>`. Stop and wait for me to approve major bumps.
Add @mycompany/ui to apps/web as workspace dep: `pnpm --filter @mycompany/web add @mycompany/ui --workspace` Then run `pnpm --filter @mycompany/web... build`.

rule.statement: Add a lint rule or grep that fails if any agent-authored file contains "npm install" or "yarn add" outside of documentation. Agents default to npm/yarn in examples.
rule.rationale: Agents trained on public code default to npm/yarn. Without an automated check, they introduce mixed package manager usage that corrupts lockfiles silently.

rule.statement: Require human approval for any major version bump or new direct dependency. Agents underweight supply-chain risk.
rule.rationale: A malicious or compromised package at a new direct dep is a supply-chain attack vector. Agent-driven bulk major upgrades bypass the review step that catches these.

antipattern.description: LLMs paste npx instead of pnpm dlx — works but bypasses pnpm's content-addressable store.
antipattern.reason: Use pnpm dlx for one-shot execution. This keeps all package resolution within pnpm's store and ensures version consistency with workspace dependencies.

antipattern.description: auto-install-peers=true masks missing peer deps; agents over-rely on it then break in strict consumers.
antipattern.reason: Keep auto-install-peers=true for convenience but periodically audit peer dependencies manually. In published packages, explicit peer deps are required.

antipattern.description: After pnpm patch, the agent commits only the patch file and forgets to commit the pnpm.patchedDependencies block in package.json.
antipattern.reason: Both the patch file and the package.json registration are required atomically. The patch file alone is ignored at install time.

antipattern.description: Native add-ons (sharp, node-canvas) sometimes need node-linker=hoisted for tools that scan node_modules flat.
antipattern.reason: Use node-linker=hoisted in .npmrc for that specific project, document the reason, and link to the upstream issue tracking the fix.

antipattern.description: Some Jest/Webpack plugins assume hoisted layout — strict mode breaks them; agents then add shamefully-hoist=true defeating the point.
antipattern.reason: Investigate the failing plugin first. The real fix is usually adding a missing peer dep or using the node-linker=hoisted exception with documentation.

antipattern.description: pnpm patch workflow is great but agents forget pnpm patch-commit and lose the diff.
antipattern.reason: After pnpm patch, always run pnpm patch-commit immediately. Commit both the patch file and the package.json pnpm.patchedDependencies block in the same commit.

antipattern.description: Lockfile churn between pnpm major versions without pinned packageManager.
antipattern.reason: Pin via "packageManager": "pnpm@9.x.y" in root package.json. Corepack enforces the same version everywhere, eliminating lockfile-format drift.

antipattern.description: workspace:* protocol doesn't work with npm publish — must use pnpm publish which rewrites it.
antipattern.reason: Always publish workspace packages with pnpm publish or pnpm -r publish. Never use npm publish for packages with workspace:* dependencies.

template.purpose: Strict .npmrc config (engine-strict, frozen lockfile, no shameful hoist)
Workspace definition for apps/packages/tools monorepo layout
GitHub Actions workflow with pnpm store cache and frozen-lockfile install
Multi-stage Dockerfile using pnpm fetch for layer caching
